candidates.item: As certified general contractors of technology, we strategically plan & execute our innovative solutions to delivery world-class hospitality tech solutions.
candidates.why_interesting: They frame themselves as the 'GC of technology' — a construction-industry analogy deliberately chosen to describe their role during hotel new-builds: coordinating all IT vendors, reviewing their plans against brand standards, and staying on as the managed IT partner after the doors open. It's a specific, concrete positioning metaphor that most IT MSPs don't use, and it signals a very particular go-to-market (new-construction projects) that competitors rarely lead with.
candidates.recency_note: Appears on about page and blog; consistent across multiple pages

candidates.item: Established since 1996 with over 2 decades of experience, we are an innovation hub of hospitality technology offering hotels breakthrough solutions for their complex needs.
candidates.why_interesting: Their own website states 1996 as founding year, but LinkedIn lists 2011 — a 15-year discrepancy that is verifiably unresolved across public sources. It's a curious internal inconsistency that could be a real conversation opener (e.g., which story is correct — did the company rebrand or restructure?). Unusual for a company to have such a large gap unexplained publicly.
candidates.recency_note: Discrepancy between advhtech.com (1996) and LinkedIn (2011) is current and unresolved
candidates.sensitivity: mild — could be interpreted as a credibility flag; use carefully

candidates.item: We adapt our solutions to fit your business model and brand requirements, whether you're operating a 30-room boutique or managing 3000+ rooms across locations.
candidates.why_interesting: The specific range — 30-room boutique to 3,000+ room multi-property chains — is an unusually wide stated scope for a niche hospitality IT firm. Most boutique IT MSPs pick a lane. Claiming both ends simultaneously is a bold positioning choice worth exploring: how does one team actually serve both without one suffering?
candidates.recency_note: Live homepage copy as of July 2026

candidates.item: Our esteemed clients include Hilton, Hyatt, Four Points, Choice Hotels International, Starwood, InterContinental Hotels Group, Independent Hotels, Best Western and Hotel Management Companies.
candidates.why_interesting: They claim a client roster that spans multiple competing major flag families simultaneously (Hilton, Hyatt, IHG, Starwood/Marriott family). Serving rival brands' compliance requirements at the same time — each with their own brand S&C standards — is a genuinely specific operational challenge that most general MSPs wouldn't touch. It validates the 'brand standards' positioning as more than marketing.
candidates.recency_note: From a 2023 blog post; client relationships may have changed

candidates.item: We have direct escalation channels with common third-party providers (e.g., PMS, POS, CCs, GPNS, GRE, phones, etc) to resolve issues swiftly and efficiently.
candidates.why_interesting: Claiming pre-established direct escalation paths with third-party vendors — PMS, POS, credit card processors, GPNS, GRE — is a specific operational claim, not a generic one. For a hotel where a PMS outage during check-in peak hours is catastrophic, 'we have a direct line' vs. 'we open a ticket' is a meaningful and verifiable differentiator.
candidates.recency_note: Live homepage copy as of July 2026

candidates.item: In this episode of 'Reshape Hospitality with Tech' we were joined by Mike Blake, Chief Technology Officer at the American Hotel & Lodging Association (AHLA).
candidates.why_interesting: A small Fremont-based IT MSP producing a podcast that lands the CTO of AHLA (the industry's primary trade association) as a guest is a legitimately surprising reach for a company of their size and profile. It signals they have genuine industry access and credibility beyond what their marketing footprint might suggest.
candidates.recency_note: Episode published September 2023; podcast appears ongoing

candidates.item: Modernize your property's operations, elevate guest experiences, and maximize ROI with customized technology consulting designed specifically for Select Service, Preferred Service, Full Service and Resort properties.
candidates.why_interesting: Using the hotel industry's own internal property-tier taxonomy (Select Service, Preferred Service, Full Service, Resort) rather than generic SMB/enterprise language signals genuine sector fluency. Most IT consultants don't know — or use — these distinctions. It's a small but telling signal of deep vertical specialization.
candidates.recency_note: Page last noted November 2025; confirmed live July 2026

candidates.item: We recommend solutions based purely on what's best for your business — not based on vendor partnerships or commissions.
candidates.why_interesting: An explicit, public claim of commission-free, vendor-agnostic recommendations is notable because they also operate a hardware resale shop (shop.advhtech.com) selling Dell, Microsoft Surface, and other gear. The combination — 'we don't take commissions' alongside a procurement/resale arm — creates a genuine tension worth asking about.
candidates.sensitivity: mild — the hardware shop creates an apparent contradiction with the vendor-agnostic claim; raise gently
